content.problemStatement:
A blood drive aims to collect NNN number of blood donations.

The drive has collected XXX donations so far.
Find the remaining number of donations needed to reach the target.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of required donations and the number of collected donations, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the remaining number of donations needed to reach the target.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and X, where N is the target number of donations and X is the number already collected.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: the remaining number of donations needed (N − X).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Need 5‑2 = 3 more donations.
Test case 2: Need 3‑3 = 0 more donations.
Test case 3: Need 5‑4 = 1 more donation.
Test case 4: Need 7‑5 = 2 more donations.
Only one test case: 10‑1 = 9 donations are still required.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply compute the difference between the required donations N and the already collected donations X. The answer is N - X. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time subtraction per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        X = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(N - X))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
